summary: Both round-1 MAJOR findings are closed with meaningful, mutation-verified assertions. MAJOR #1 (header/data alignment, Decision 5) is now guarded by Test_render_alignmentInvariant, a genuine ANSI-stripped visible-width invariant that provably fails on the pre-fix behavior (verified by mutation: header=38 vs data=36, the 2-marker delta) and passes after the fix. MAJOR #2 (left marker at mid-offset) is covered by Test_printStatHeader_midOffset_bothMarkers, which fails when left-marker emission is suppressed. All three round-1 minors are closed: frozen-bold now asserts the exact escape sequence (\033[30;47;1mcol0), the right-marker is asserted at end-of-line via HasSuffix, and the truncation branch is exercised. Test_visibleColumns was correctly updated for the new 1-cell marker reservation: the missing/zero case last 4->3 is a real behavioral change (budget 28->27 drops col4), not a tautology. The columnWindow / renderDbstat refactor introduced no coverage gaps that matter: absolute-index lookup, clamp write-back, empty-result, and both edge markers are all covered and mutation-sensitive. One MINOR edge gap remains (empty scroll window + render printers), recorded as tech_debt.

findings.issue: The empty scrollable window case (win.last < win.first, only the frozen column fits, hiddenRight=true) is tested only at the visibleColumns unit level. The render printers (printStatHeader/printStatData) are never exercised with an empty window. In that state the header must print the frozen column + right marker with NO scrollable columns, and every data row must reserve the matching right-marker blank. The alignment-invariant litmus does not cover this branch because it uses a mid-offset window with visible scrollable columns. A regression where the empty-window data row drops the right-marker filler (misalignment with header) would not be caught.
findings.recommendation: Add a subtest rendering both header and data with termWidth=12 (only frozen col0 fits) for a 6-column result: assert header contains col0 and the right marker, asserts col1.. are absent, and that visibleRuneLen(header)==visibleRuneLen(each data row). Reuse makeRenderConfig/makeRenderResult and visibleRuneLen already present in the file.

findings.location: top/stat_test.go:837 Test_printStatHeader_frozenColumn/sort highlight has priority on column 0
findings.issue: The second assertion (strings.Count over out[:index(col0)+len(col0)] == 1) is a weak, slightly obscure guard against doubled escape sequences. It counts occurrences of the literal 'col0' in a prefix slice, which will always be 1 by construction of the prefix and does not actually prove the frozen-bold sequence is absent. The first assertion (Contains \033[47;1mcol0) carries the real signal; the second adds confusion without protecting against the doubling it claims to.
findings.recommendation: Replace the count assertion with a direct negative: assert.NotContains(t, out, "\033[30;47;1mcol0") to prove the frozen-bold escape is NOT applied when OrderKey==0 (sort highlight wins, Decision 4). This directly verifies non-doubling.

mutationVerified: Test_render_alignmentInvariant: zero-width data fillers -> header 38 vs data 36, FAIL (MAJOR #1 closed)
Test_printStatHeader_midOffset_bothMarkers + alignmentInvariant: suppress left marker -> FAIL (MAJOR #2 closed)
Test_printStatData_windowed_midOffset + alignmentInvariant: data loop ignores offset (i:=1) -> FAIL (absolute-index lookup guarded)

round1FindingsStatus.major_2_left_marker_midOffset: closed — Test_printStatHeader_midOffset_bothMarkers, fails when left marker suppressed
round1FindingsStatus.minor_truncation_branch: closed — Test_printStatData_truncation asserts abcd~ present, abcde absent

refactorRegressionCheck.renderDbstat_split: covered — Test_printDbstat_clampsScrollOffset drives renderDbstat with synthetic termWidth
refactorRegressionCheck.Test_visibleColumns_marker_reservation_u: non-tautological — missing/zero last 4->3 reflects real 1-cell budget reduction; values recomputed and correct
